title: drymocallis glandulosa subsp. pseudorupestris
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. pseudorupestris, also known as Western bitterroot, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Western bitterroot include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Western bitterroot can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a valuable resource in natural remedies.
